title: WORKSTATION1 Workflow Backup
tagline_grad: Your workflow state, safely on ZFS.
lead: A WSL systemd user timer that rsyncs repos, Hermes AI state, SSH keys, and Windows workflow artifacts to a restricted TrueNAS identity every hour — with a five-layer safety design and silent success.
sub: Fail-closed growth guard, pinned-host-key SSH, consistent SQLite snapshots, SHA-256 integrity manifest, and a ZFS snapshot ladder retaining up to one year of rollback history. Telegram alert fires only on failure.
features_intro: Eight capabilities across hourly automation, security hardening, and structured restore — all fail-closed and silent on success.

features.title: Hourly silent sync
features.body: systemd user timer fires at :45 every hour. rsync mirrors WSL paths; robocopy mirrors Windows artifacts over SMB. Successful runs emit nothing.

features.title: Consistent SQLite snapshots
features.body: SQLite backup API snapshots Hermes state.db, kanban.db, lifelog, and browser-memory DBs before the rsync phase — never copies a live, write-in-progress database.

features.title: Fail-closed growth guard
features.body: Checks dataset used (≤2 TiB), snapshot-held blocks (≤1 TiB), free space (≥2 TiB), and snapshot count (≤5 000) before and after writes. Stops backup; never deletes.

features.title: Restricted runtime identity
features.body: Hourly path uses dedicated user ws1backup with pinned host key (StrictHostKeyChecking=yes, HostKeyAlias) and a forced-command SSH dispatcher that rejects arbitrary shell, PTY, and rsync sender mode.

features.title: Run ledger + integrity manifest
features.body: Every run appends to a SQLite event ledger. On success, SHA-256 checksums for critical restore artifacts are written to NAS under current/_manifests/integrity-manifest.json.

features.title: Failure-only Telegram alert
features.body: systemd OnFailure unit sends a Telegram message via the Hermes bot on every nonzero service exit. Zero noise on success; immediate signal on failure.

features.title: ZFS snapshot ladder
features.body: TrueNAS periodic snapshot tasks retain: hourly 2 days, daily 2 weeks, weekly 8 weeks, monthly 1 year. No repo-owned zfs destroy helper.

features.title: Structured restore path
features.body: Restore runbook covers missing repo files, Hermes state.db, Windows artifacts, and staged restore with integrity-manifest checksum verification before replacing live state.

flow_intro: The same guarded path runs every hour — pre-flight, data movement, post-flight, publication.

flow.title: Timer fires at :45
flow.body: workstation-workflow-backup.timer triggers the service via systemd user session

flow.title: Pre-flight growth guard
flow.body: check-nas-growth-guard.sh --stage pre: fails closed if dataset used, snapshot blocks, free space, or snapshot count exceeds budget

flow.title: Consistent SQLite snapshots
flow.body: snapshot-sqlite-dbs.py uses the SQLite backup API before any rsync — ensures consistent copies of live Hermes, lifelog, and browser-memory DBs

flow.title: WSL rsync to NAS
flow.body: rsync over restricted runtime SSH (ws1backup, pinned host key, forced-command) writes ~/repos, ~/.hermes, ~/.ssh, and other paths to NAS current/wsl/

flow.title: Windows robocopy over SMB
flow.body: sync-windows-critical.ps1 via PowerShell rsyncs Desktop, Documents, .ssh, Terminal, VS Code, Chrome metadata to NAS current/windows/

flow.title: Post-flight growth guard
flow.body: check-nas-growth-guard.sh --stage post: re-checks all budgets after writes; fails closed if exceeded

flow.title: Ledger completion + manifest
flow.body: record-run-ledger.py records completed event; nas-integrity-manifest-helper.py writes SHA-256 checksums for critical restore artifacts to NAS

commands_intro: Four functional groups. Read-only ops first; provisioning and restore require admin identity.
model_intro: Everything flows through one restricted path on a ZFS snapshot ladder with a fail-closed guard.

spokes.label: Ledger + alerts
spokes.sub: run events + SHA-256 manifest + Telegram OnFailure

model_cap: Restricted runtime identity (ws1backup). Admin SSH required only for provisioning, restore, and snapshot inspection.
audiences_intro: Three doors in, by what you need right now.

audiences.title: Executive Brief
audiences.body: Risk posture, retention schedule, and maturity summary — no code required.

audiences.title: User Guide
audiences.body: First-run journey, I want to… routing table, failure alert response, and the one safety rule.

audiences.title: Architecture & ADRs
audiences.body: C4 model, 10 views, 6 ADRs, runtime hardening decisions, and design rationale.

boundary_intro: The hourly runtime path enforces a hard boundary: restricted identity, no root, pinned host key, fail-closed guards.
boundary_cap: The runtime SSH path is forced-command only: no interactive shell, no PTY, no rsync sender mode, no port forwarding.
boundary_note: Root/admin SSH is required only for explicit provisioning, snapshot inspection, and restore. See ADR-0006.
docmap_intro: Docs are maintained in Markdown (source of truth) with generated HTML companions. The front door links to styled HTML; GitHub renders the Markdown natively.
footer_provenance: Generated from workstation-workflow-backup — 14 test functions across 1 test file.
